name: Credential Management
summary: Conformance reference for acquiring and managing a user's credentials behind one swappable seam — the navigator.credentials dispatcher normalized as a CustomCredentialProvider (default → project override → custom plug), with passkey/WebAuthn, FedCM, digital-credential, and password as families on one request dimension (credentials:[…]). The provider contract is the only lock: async, server-authoritative (the browser only gathers; the server verifies), and revocable — Guard's contract verbatim. Dispatch is 2-D: fan a request by family AND by member. The lifecycle is an OPEN member set, not a flat four-method surface (which would force get-only families to stub create/store): credential-request (get — all families), credential-enrollment (create/store — passkey + password), session-mediation (preventSilentAccess sign-out — origin-global). Default provider = native navigator.credentials passthrough; a mock in-memory conformance provider ships in the first slice so every downstream surface is demoable without an RP server. Ceremony libraries (SimpleWebAuthn/Hanko) are swappable plugs. Identity produces the auth-state predicate Guard's access-gate consumes (#272). Ruled by #496; built in #483.
